items.title: Trump brings up Pearl Harbor while meeting with Japanese prime minister
items.standfirst: President Trump met with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in the Oval Office on Thursday and invoked Pearl Harbor when a reporter asked why he didn't give U.S. allies advanced notice before the initial strikes against Iran. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has more.

clusters.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar

items.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar
items.standfirst: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.
items.content: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. The decision follows complaints from the host nation Moroccan, after a highly contested final. The news has left many Senegalese in shock, torn between confusion and anger, as FRANCE 24’s team in Dakar reports.

items.title: Senegal instruct lawyers, while CAF’s Motsepe defends Morocco’s AFCON award
items.standfirst: Senegal football calls in lawyers as CAS awaits, while CAF president defends decision to hand AFCON title to Morocco.
items.content: Senegal football calls in lawyers as CAS awaits, while CAF president defends decision to hand AFCON title to Morocco.

items.title: Senegal to appeal CAF Africa Cup of Nations decision after team stripped of title
items.standfirst: Senegal's football federation said Thursday that it would appeal the Confederation of African Football (CAF) decision to strip the country's team of its Africa Cup of Nations and award the title to Morocco. Senegal beat Morocco in Rabat on January 18, a victory that was short-lived after CAF reversed Senegal's win two months later.
items.content: Senegal's football federation said Thursday that it would appeal the Confederation of African Football (CAF) decision to strip the country's team of its Africa Cup of Nations and award the title to Morocco. Senegal beat Morocco in Rabat on January 18, a victory that was short-lived after CAF reversed Senegal's win two months later.

items.content: The football federation of Morocco welcomes the CAF Appeal Board decision to award it the 2025 AFCON title.

items.content: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.

items.title: Trump says U.S. doesn't need help defending Strait of Hormuz after allies refuse
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump dropped his push for U.S. allies to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian threats — an about-face that came just one day after he called upon nations to “get involved” so oil tankers can safely navigate the crucial shipping lane.

clusters.title: Hegseth says no ‘timeframe’ for war on Iran as Pentagon asks for $200bn

items.title: Hegseth says no ‘timeframe’ for war on Iran as Pentagon asks for $200bn
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hints war funding may change, leaving timeline decisions to President Trump.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hints war funding may change, leaving timeline decisions to President Trump.

items.title: REPLAY: Pentagon press conference on war in Middle East
items.standfirst: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday there is no "time frame" for ending the US-Israeli war against Iran, which was launched three weeks ago. "We wouldn't want to set a definitive time frame," Hegseth told a news conference, adding that "we're very much on track" and that President Donald Trump will be the one to decide when to stop. Hegseth also addressed a report that the Pentagon has requested more than $200 billion in additional funding from Congress to pay for the conflict.
items.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday there is no "time frame" for ending the US-Israeli war against Iran, which was launched three weeks ago. "We wouldn't want to set a definitive time frame," Hegseth told a news conference, adding that "we're very much on track" and that President Donald Trump will be the one to decide when to stop. Hegseth also addressed a report that the Pentagon has requested more than $200 billion in additional funding from Congress to pay for the conflict.

ai_summary: Pentagon requests additional funding
Funding to support war with Iran
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth hints at timeline
No definitive timeframe for war end
President Trump to decide on war end

clusters.title: As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war

items.title: As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war
items.standfirst: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.
items.content: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.(Image credit: Fazelminallah Qazizai for NPR)

items.title: Pakistan to pause Afghan strikes for Eid, two days after deadly Kabul attack
items.standfirst: Five-day cessation announced as mass funeral held for some of hundreds of victims of airstrike on rehab centrePakistan has announced a five-day pause in strikes against neighbouring Afghanistan, as a mass funeral was held for some of the hundreds of victims killed in Monday’s attack on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul.The Afghan Taliban government has said more than 400 people were killed and 265 others wounded in that attack, which took place as people at the centre were praying days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan. Continue reading...
items.content: Five-day cessation announced as mass funeral held for some of hundreds of victims of airstrike on rehab centrePakistan has announced a five-day pause in strikes against neighbouring Afghanistan, as a mass funeral was held for some of the hundreds of victims killed in Monday’s attack on a drug rehabilitation centre in Kabul.The Afghan Taliban government has said more than 400 people were killed and 265 others wounded in that attack, which took place as people at the centre were praying days before the end of the holy month of Ramadan. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Pakistan and Afghanistan agree to truce
Truce to last for five days
